title.zh: 卷二十九 申屠剛鮑永郅惲列傳
title.en: Volume 29: Biographies of Shentu Gang; Bao Yong; Zhi Yun
title.raw: 卷二十九 申屠剛鮑永郅惲列傳 Volume 29: Biographies of Shentu Gang; Bao Yong; Zhi Yun

sentences.zh: 申屠剛

translations.literal: Shentu Gang.
translations.idiomatic: Shentu Gang

sentences.zh: 申屠剛字巨卿，扶風茂陵人也。

translations.literal: Shentu Gang's style was Juqing; he was a man of Maoling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Shentu Gang, courtesy name Juqing, came from Maoling in Fufeng commandery.

sentences.zh: 七世祖嘉，文帝時為丞相。

translations.literal: His seventh-generation ancestor Jia was Chancellor under Emperor Wen.
translations.idiomatic: Seven generations back, his ancestor Shentu Jia had served as chancellor under Emperor Wen of Han.

sentences.zh: 剛質性方直，常慕史䲡、汲黯之為人。

translations.literal: Gang by nature was square and upright; he constantly admired the conduct of Shi Qiu and Ji An.
translations.idiomatic: He was blunt and honest by temperament and modeled himself on Shi Qiu and Ji An.

sentences.zh: 仕郡功曹。

translations.literal: He served as Merit Assessor of the commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He served as merit assessor for his commandery.

sentences.zh: 平帝時，王莽專政，朝多猜忌，遂隔絕帝外家馮、衛二族，不得交宦，剛常疾之。

translations.literal: At the time of Emperor Ping, Wang Mang monopolized government; the court was full of suspicion and jealousy; therefore he cut off the emperor's maternal kin, the Feng and Wei two clans, from contact with officials—Gang constantly hated this.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Ping, Wang Mang controlled the court and bred suspicion everywhere; he barred the emperor's maternal relatives of the Feng and Wei houses from office—something Gang bitterly resented.

sentences.zh: 及舉賢良方正，因對策曰：

translations.literal: When he was recommended Worthy and Good and Upright, he responded to the policy questions, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as worthy, upright, and good, he answered the examination question with this memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞王事失則神祇怨怨，奸邪亂正，故陰陽謬錯，此天所以譴告王者，欲令失道之君，曠然覺悟，懷邪之臣，懼然自刻者也。

translations.literal: Your minister has heard that when royal affairs fail, then spirits and gods resent resentfully; wickedness and evil disorder the upright—therefore yin and yang are wrong and tangled—this is Heaven's reason for reprimanding and warning the king; it desires to make the lord who has lost the Way suddenly awaken, and make wicked ministers fearful and self-scrutinizing.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant has heard that when royal government fails, the spirits grow resentful and treason drives out integrity, so yin and yang fall out of joint. Heaven sends such warnings to wake a ruler who has strayed from the Way and to strike fear into wicked ministers.

sentences.zh: 今朝廷不考功校德，而虛納毀譽，數下詔書，張設重法，抑斷誹謗，禁割論議，罪之重者，乃至腰斬。

translations.literal: Now the court does not examine merit and compare virtue, but emptily accepts slander and praise; repeatedly issuing edicts, spreading severe laws, suppressing and cutting off slander, forbidding and severing discussion—for heavy crimes, even cutting at the waist.
translations.idiomatic: Today the court weighs neither merit nor character but swallows praise and blame wholesale; edicts pile up, harsh laws multiply, dissent is silenced and debate outlawed—the worst offenses earn death by waist-cutting.

sentences.zh: 傷忠臣之情，挫直士之銳，殆乖建進善之旌，縣敢諫之鼓，辟四門之路，明四目之義也。

translations.literal: It wounds loyal ministers' feelings, blunts upright gentlemen's keen edge—almost contradicting raising the banner of advancing good, hanging the drum of daring remonstrance, opening the four gates' roads, clarifying the four eyes' meaning.
translations.idiomatic: That breaks the hearts of loyal men and dulls the edge of the outspoken—quite at odds with raising the standard for good counsel, hanging the drum for bold remonstrance, opening the four gates, and emulating the far seeing of the four eyes.

sentences.zh: 昔周公先遣伯禽守封於魯，以義寒恩，寵不加後，故配天郊祀，三十餘世。

translations.literal: Formerly the Duke of Zhou first sent Boqin to guard the fief in Lu—by righteousness he chilled favor; favor did not increase for later generations—therefore he matched Heaven at suburban sacrifice, thirty-odd generations.
translations.idiomatic: Long ago the Duke of Zhou sent Boqin to Lu: duty came before private affection, so favor did not pile up for his kin—and that house sacrificed to Heaven by the suburbs for more than thirty generations.

sentences.zh: 霍光秉政，輔翼少主，修善進士，名為忠直，而尊崇其宗黨，摧抑外戚，結貴據權，至堅至固，終沒之後，受禍滅門。

translations.literal: Huo Guang held government, aided the young lord, cultivated goodness and advanced gentlemen—named loyal and upright—yet he honored and exalted his clan party, crushed and suppressed outer kin, tied to the noble and grasped power—most firm and solid; after he finally died, they suffered disaster and extermination of the whole gate.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Guang governed for the boy emperor, promoted good men, and passed for loyal—yet he exalted his own clan, crushed the imperial in-laws, bound himself to power so tightly that when he died his whole line was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 方今師傅皆以伊、周之位，據賢保之任，以此思化，則功何不至？

translations.literal: Now the tutors and guardians all hold Yi Yin and Duke of Zhou's positions, occupy the duty of protecting worthies—by this thinking of transformation, how could achievement not arrive?
translations.idiomatic: Your tutors and guardians stand in the place of Yi Yin and the Duke of Zhou and bear the charge to protect wise counsel—if they truly taught reform, what goal could be beyond reach?

sentences.zh: 不思其危，則禍何不到？

translations.literal: If they do not think of its peril, how could disaster not arrive?
translations.idiomatic: If they ignore the danger, how can ruin stay away?

sentences.zh: 損益之際，孔父攸嘆，持滿之戒，老氏所慎。

translations.literal: At the juncture of diminishing and increasing, Kong Fu sighed; the warning of holding fullness—what Laozi cautioned.
translations.idiomatic: At the turning between gain and loss Kongzi sighed; holding a full cup—what Laozi warned against.

sentences.zh: 蓋功冠天下者不安，威震人主者不全。

translations.literal: For merit crowning all under Heaven is not secure; awe shaking the human lord is not preserved.
translations.idiomatic: Whoever towers above the realm cannot rest easy; whoever terrifies his ruler cannot stay whole.

sentences.zh: 今承衰亂之後，繼重敝之世，公家屈竭，賦斂重數，苛吏奪其時，貪夫侵其財，百姓困乏，疾疫夭命。

translations.literal: Now inheriting after decline and chaos, succeeding a generation heavy with exhaustion—the ducal houses are drained and exhausted; levies and taxes are heavy and repeated; harsh clerks seize their seasons; greedy men encroach their wealth; the hundred surnames are destitute; plague and sickness cut short lives.
translations.idiomatic: We stand on the ruins of turmoil: the treasury is dry, taxes multiply, harsh clerks steal farmers' seasons and predators seize their goods; common families starve and pestilence carries off the young.

sentences.zh: 盜賊群輩，且以萬數，軍行眾止，竊號自立，攻犯京師，燔燒縣邑，至乃訛言積弩入宮，宿衛驚懼。

translations.literal: Bandits swarm in groups—almost numbering ten thousand—armies march and masses halt; they steal titles and establish themselves; they attack and violate the capital region; they burn counties and cities—to the extent of false rumor that massed crossbows entered the palace; night guards were frightened and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: Brigands gather by the tens of thousands; armies march and halts multiply; rebels proclaim themselves and strike toward the capital; towns burn until rumor says crossbows have entered the palace and the night guard trembles.

sentences.zh: 自漢興以來，誠未有也。

translations.literal: Since Han arose, truly there has never been such.
translations.idiomatic: Since the founding of Han nothing like this has been seen.

sentences.zh: 國家微弱，奸謀不禁，六極之效，危於累卵。

translations.literal: The state is weak and slight; treacherous plots are not forbidden—the effect of the six extremes is more perilous than a pile of eggs.
translations.idiomatic: The dynasty is feeble and intrigue unchecked—the six extremes threaten us like eggs stacked high.

sentences.zh: 王者承天順地，典爵主刑，不敢以天官私其宗，不敢以天罰輕其親。

translations.literal: The king receives Heaven and follows earth; he regulates ranks and administers punishments—he does not dare to privatize Heaven's offices for his clan; he does not dare lightly to apply Heaven's punishments to his kin.
translations.idiomatic: The true king receives his mandate from Heaven and walks the earth's pattern; he sets ranks and wields punishment—yet he does not treat heavenly office as a family monopoly nor lighten Heaven's sentence for kin.

sentences.zh: 陛下宜遂聖明之德，昭然覺悟，遠述帝王之跡，近遵孝文之業，差五品之屬，納至親之序，亟遣使者征中山太後，置之別官，令時朝見。

translations.literal: Your Majesty ought to complete sagely enlightened virtue, clearly awaken, far trace emperors and kings' tracks, near follow Emperor Xiaowen's enterprise; arrange the five grades' kin, admit utmost kin's order; urgently dispatch envoys to summon the Dowager of Zhongshan, place her in a separate palace, command timely court audiences.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty should recover enlightened rule: look far to the ancient kings and near to Emperor Wen—order the five grades of kin, restore precedence among close relatives, send envoys at once to bring the Queen Dowager of Zhongshan to a separate residence, and let her appear at court on schedule.

sentences.zh: 又召馮、衛二族，裁與冗職，使得執戟，親奉宿衛，以防未然之符，以抑患禍之端，上安社稷，下全保傅，內和親戚，外絕邪謀。

translations.literal: Further summon the Feng and Wei two clans, assign them only supernumerary posts, let them hold halberds, personally attend night guard—to guard against omens not yet manifest, to suppress the beginnings of disaster and misfortune—above pacifying altars of soil and grain, below completing tutors and guardians; within harmonizing kin, without cutting off wicked plots.
translations.idiomatic: Recall the Feng and Wei houses to nominal posts and halberd duty in the night guard—forestall what has not yet happened and choke off calamity at the source: secure the state above, protect your tutors below, bind kin within, and foil intrigue without.

sentences.zh: 書奏，莽令元後下詔曰：『剛聽言僻經妄說，違背大義。

translations.literal: When the document was submitted, Mang ordered the Yuan Empress Dowager to issue an edict saying: 'Gang listened to words warped from the classics and wild speech—he violates great righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial went up, Wang Mang made Grand Empress Dowager Wang issue an edict: "Shentu Gang spouts twisted learning and wild doctrine; he offends the great norm.

sentences.zh: 其罷歸田裏。』

translations.literal: Let him be dismissed and return to the fields.'
translations.idiomatic: Dismiss him to his fields."

sentences.zh: 後莽篡位，剛遂避地河西，轉入巴、蜀，往來二十許年。

translations.literal: Later Mang usurped the throne; Gang then fled his ground to Hexi, shifted into Ba and Shu—coming and going about twenty years.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang seized the throne Gang withdrew west of the river, then moved between Ba and Shu for some twenty years.

sentences.zh: 及隗囂據隴右，欲背漢而附公孫述。

translations.literal: When Wei Xiao held Longyou and wished to turn from Han and attach to Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: When Wei Xiao controlled Longyou he meant to abandon Han for Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 剛說之曰：『愚聞人所歸者天所與，人所畔者天所去也。

translations.literal: Gang admonished him, saying: 'Your dull servant has heard that whom men return to—Heaven grants; whom men rebel against—Heaven casts off.
translations.idiomatic: Gang urged him: "I have heard that Heaven favors those whom men follow and casts off those whom men desert.

sentences.zh: 伏念本朝躬聖德，舉義兵，龔行天罰，所當必摧，誠天之所福，非人力也。

translations.literal: Your minister humbly reflects that our dynasty in person embodies sagely virtue, raises righteous troops, respectfully carries out Heaven's punishment—where it strikes must be shattered—truly Heaven's blessing, not human force.
translations.idiomatic: Our house embodies sacred virtue, raises righteous arms, and executes Heaven's judgment—what it strikes must break; that is Heaven's favor, not mere manpower.

sentences.zh: 將軍本無尺土，孤立一隅，宜推誠奉順，與朝並力，上應天心，下酬人望，為國立功，可以永年。

translations.literal: The general originally had not a foot of soil—stood alone in one corner—ought to extend sincerity and serve submission, join strength with the court; above respond to Heaven's heart, below repay men's hopes; for the state establish merit—can thereby prolong years.
translations.idiomatic: You began without an inch of territory and stand isolated—yield in good faith, march with the court, answer Heaven above and the people below, and win lasting merit for the state.

sentences.zh: 嫌疑之事，聖人所絕。

translations.literal: Matters of suspicion—the sages cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Suspicion is what sages cut short.

sentences.zh: 以將軍之威重，遠在千里，動作舉措，可不慎與？

translations.literal: By the general's awesome weight—far at a thousand li—movements and actions—can one not be cautious?
translations.idiomatic: At your distance and stature every move is watched—how can you afford carelessness?

sentences.zh: 今璽書數到，委國歸信，欲與將軍共同吉兇。

translations.literal: Now imperial rescripts have repeatedly arrived, entrusting the state and returning trust—desiring to share good and ill fortune with the general.
translations.idiomatic: Rescripts have come again and again, offering the state's trust and asking to share fortune and disaster with you.

sentences.zh: 布衣相與，尚有沒身不負然諾之信，況於萬乘者哉！

translations.literal: Common cloth mutually associate—still there is not betraying life-long pledge faith—how much more for one of ten thousand chariots!
translations.idiomatic: Commoners who shake hands still keep a promise for life—how much more a ruler of ten thousand chariots?

sentences.zh: 今何畏何利，久疑如是？

translations.literal: Now what fear, what profit—long doubtful thus?
translations.idiomatic: What fear, what gain keeps you hesitating so long?

sentences.zh: 卒有非常之變，上負忠孝，下愧當世。

translations.literal: If finally there is extraordinary change—above you betray loyalty and filiality; below you shame the present age.
translations.idiomatic: If crisis strikes you will fail loyalty and filial piety above and bring shame on your age below.

sentences.zh: 夫未至豫言，固常為虛，及其已至，又無所及，是以忠言至諫，希得為用。

translations.literal: What has not yet arrived—if foretold beforehand—is normally empty; when it has arrived—again nothing can catch up—therefore loyal words and utmost remonstrance—rarely get to be used.
translations.idiomatic: Warnings before disaster sound hollow; once calamity hits it is too late—so honest counsel is seldom heeded.

sentences.zh: 誠願反復愚老之言。

translations.literal: Truly I wish you would turn over again this dull old man's words.
translations.idiomatic: Please reconsider what this foolish old man has said.

sentences.zh: 』囂不納，遂畔從述。

translations.literal: ' Xiao did not accept—thereupon rebelled and followed Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao refused and turned to Shu.

sentences.zh: 建武七年，詔書征剛。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Jianwu, an edict summoned Gang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 7 an edict recalled Gang.

sentences.zh: 剛將歸，與囂書曰：『愚聞專己者孤，拒諫者塞，孤塞之政，亡國之風也。

translations.literal: Gang, about to return, wrote to Xiao, saying: 'Your dull servant has heard that those who monopolize themselves are isolated; those who reject remonstrance are blocked—isolated and blocked government is the wind of a perishing state.
translations.idiomatic: As Gang prepared to answer the summons he wrote to Wei Xiao: "I have heard that willful rulers stand alone and those who spurn advice are blocked—isolation and blockage are the breath of a dying state.

sentences.zh: 雖有明聖之姿，猶屈己從眾，故慮無遺策，舉無過事。

translations.literal: Although there is perspicacious sagely bearing—still they bend self and follow the multitude—therefore planning leaves no strategy omitted; raising affairs leaves no fault.
translations.idiomatic: Even the sage bends to the multitude so no plan is missed and no act misjudged.

sentences.zh: 夫聖人不以獨見為明，而以萬物為心。

translations.literal: The sage does not take lone insight as clarity, but takes the myriad things as heart.
translations.idiomatic: The sage does not trust private judgment but sets his heart on the needs of all.

sentences.zh: 順人者昌，逆人者亡，此古今之所共也。

translations.literal: Those who follow men flourish; those who oppose men perish—this is what ancient and modern share.
translations.idiomatic: Those who move with the people thrive; those who defy them perish—that has never changed.

sentences.zh: 將軍以布衣為鄉裏所推，廊廟之計，既不豫定，動軍發眾，又不深料。

translations.literal: The general, beginning from common cloth—was pushed by village and district—court planning—was not predetermined in advance; moving troops and mobilizing masses—was not deeply weighed.
translations.idiomatic: You rose from common recommendation without a settled strategy for the court; you raised armies without deep reckoning.

sentences.zh: 今東方政教日睦，百姓平安，而西州發兵，人人憂憂，騷動惶懼，莫敢正言，群眾疑惑，人懷顧望。

translations.literal: Now the east's government and teaching daily harmonizes—common people peaceful and calm—while the western province raises troops—every person anxious anxious—agitated and frightened—none dares speak straight—the masses are doubtful and confused—each person harbors looking back and hoping.
translations.idiomatic: The east grows orderly and the people rest easy while your province arms itself in fear; rumor spreads, none dare speak plainly, and hearts waver.

sentences.zh: 非徒無精銳之心，其患無所不至。

translations.literal: Not only is there no keen and sharp heart—their misfortune knows no limit.
translations.idiomatic: That is more than loss of nerve—the harm knows no end.

sentences.zh: 夫物窮則變生，事急則計易，其勢然也。

translations.literal: When things reach exhaustion then change is born; when affairs are urgent then plans alter—the trend is thus.
translations.idiomatic: Extremity forces change; urgency alters plans—that is how circumstance works.

sentences.zh: 夫離道德，逆人情，而能有國有家者，古今未有也。

translations.literal: Leaving morality, opposing human feeling—yet being able to have state and household—ancient and modern have never had it.
translations.idiomatic: No one who abandons the Way and flouts human hearts has ever kept state or clan.

sentences.zh: 將軍素以忠孝顯聞，是以士大夫不遠千里，慕樂德義。

translations.literal: The general always by loyalty and filiality shown and heard—therefore gentlemen did not shrink from a thousand li—admiring and delighting in virtue and righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Your fame for loyalty and duty drew scholars from a thousand li who wished to share your moral purpose.

sentences.zh: 今茍欲決意僥幸，此何如哉？

translations.literal: Now if you only desire to resolve intent on hazard's luck—how is this?
translations.idiomatic: If you now gamble everything on a reckless throw—what end do you expect?

sentences.zh: 夫天所祐者順，人所助者信。

translations.literal: What Heaven blesses is submission; what men assist is trust.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven aids the obedient; men follow the trustworthy.

sentences.zh: 如未蒙祐助，令小人受塗地之禍，毀壞終身之德，敗亂君臣之節，汙傷父子之恩，眾賢破膽，可不慎哉！

translations.literal: If you do not receive blessing and aid—ordering petty men to receive the catastrophe of smeared earth—destroying and crushing lifelong virtue—disordering and ruining lord-minister's integrity—defiling and injuring father-son's kindness—the many worthies break gall—can one not be cautious?
translations.idiomatic: Without that blessing you will doom followers to ruin, stain your life's virtue, break fealty between lord and minister and love between father and son, and terrify every good man—can you ignore the danger?

sentences.zh: 』囂不納。

translations.literal: ' Xiao did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao still refused.

sentences.zh: 剛到，拜侍御史，遷尚書令。

translations.literal: When Gang arrived, he was appointed Attendant Censor; promoted to Masters of Writing Supervisor.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he was named attendant censor and rose to supervisor of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 光武嘗欲出遊，剛以隴蜀未平，不宜宴安逸豫。

translations.literal: Guangwu once wished to go out touring—Gang because Long and Shu were not yet pacified—said it was not fitting to feast and roam at ease.
translations.idiomatic: Once Guangwu wished to tour abroad; Gang argued that Long and Shu were still unconquered and ease was unfitting.

sentences.zh: 諫不見聽，遂以頭軔乘輿輪，帝遂為止。

translations.literal: Remonstrance was not listened to—thereupon he pressed his head against the imperial carriage's wheel—the emperor thereupon stopped.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor ignored him he wedged his head against the wheel of the carriage until Guangwu gave up the outing.

sentences.zh: 時內外群官，多帝自選舉，加以法理嚴察，職事過苦，尚書近臣，乃至捶撲牽曳於前，群臣莫敢正言。

translations.literal: At the time inner and outer hosts of officials—many the emperor himself selected and promoted—plus legal principle strictly scrutinized—duties excessively bitter—Masters of Writing near ministers—even to beating and dragging before him—the throng of ministers none dared speak straight.
translations.idiomatic: Most offices were the emperor's own picks; law ran harsh and duties crushed men; imperial secretaries were beaten before the throne while the court stayed silent.

sentences.zh: 剛每輒極諫，又數言皇太子宜時就東宮，簡任賢保，以成其德，帝並不納。

translations.literal: Gang each time utterly remonstrated—also repeatedly said the imperial heir ought timely to go to the eastern palace—select and appoint worthy guardians—to complete his virtue—the emperor together did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: Gang remonstrated to the limit and urged again that the heir enter the eastern palace with worthy tutors—Guangwu accepted none of it.

sentences.zh: 以數切諫失旨，數年，出為平陰令。

translations.literal: Because repeatedly cutting remonstrance missed intent—several years—sent out as Magistrate of Pingyin.
translations.idiomatic: After years of blunt advice that missed the ruler's mood he was sent out as magistrate of Pingyin.

sentences.zh: 復征拜太中大夫，以病去官，卒於家。

translations.literal: Again summoned and appointed Grand Palace Grandee—because of illness left office—died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He was later recalled as grand palace grandee but resigned ill and died at home.

sentences.zh: 鮑永字君長，上黨屯留人也。

translations.literal: Bao Yong's style was Junzhang—he was a man of Tunliu in Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Yong, courtesy name Junzhang, was a native of Tunliu in Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 父宣，哀帝時任司隸校尉，為王莽所殺。

translations.literal: Father Xuan—at the time of Emperor Ai served as Metropolitan Superintendent—was killed by Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: His father Bao Xuan had been metropolitan superintendent under Emperor Ai until Wang Mang had him executed.

sentences.zh: 永少有誌操，習歐陽《尚書》。

translations.literal: Yong from youth had intent and integrity—studied Ouyang's Documents.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood Yong had resolve and studied the Ouyang school of the Documents.

sentences.zh: 事後母至孝，妻嘗於母前叱狗，而永即去之。

translations.literal: Serving his stepmother utmost filiality—wife once before mother scolded the dog—and Yong immediately divorced her.
translations.idiomatic: He was utterly filial to his stepmother; when his wife shouted at a dog in her presence he divorced her at once.

sentences.zh: 初為郡功曹。

translations.literal: First he served as commandery Merit Assessor.
translations.idiomatic: He began as merit assessor for the commandery.

sentences.zh: 莽以宣不附己，欲不其子孫。

translations.literal: Because Xuan did not attach to himself—Mang wished to cut off his sons and grandsons.
translations.idiomatic: Because Bao Xuan had defied him, Wang Mang meant to destroy his descendants.

sentences.zh: 都尉路平承望風旨，規欲害永。

translations.literal: Commandant Lu Ping accepted lookout for intent—planned wishing to harm Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Commandant Lu Ping, catching Wang Mang's drift, plotted against Yong.

sentences.zh: 太守茍諫擁護，召以為吏，常置府中，永因數為諫陳興復漢室，剪滅篡逆之策。

translations.literal: Governor Gou Jian embraced and protected—summoned him to be clerk—constantly placed him in the mansion—Yong therefore repeatedly for Jian stated policies for restoring Han house and cutting down usurpation and rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Gou Jian shielded him, took him on staff, and kept him at headquarters; Yong repeatedly urged him to restore the Han and destroy the usurper.

sentences.zh: 諫每戒永曰：『君長幾事不密，禍倚人門。

translations.literal: Jian constantly warned Yong, saying: 'Junzhang—several affairs not secret—misfortune leans on men's gates.
translations.idiomatic: Jian often warned him: "Junzhang, when plans leak, disaster walks through your door.

sentences.zh: 』永感其言。

translations.literal: ' Yong was moved by his words.
translations.idiomatic: Yong took the warning to heart.

sentences.zh: 及諫卒，自送喪歸扶風，路平遂收永弟升。

translations.literal: When Jian died—personally escorting the funeral returning to Fufeng—Lu Ping thereupon seized Yong's younger brother Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: When Jian died Yong escorted the body to Fufeng; Lu Ping seized his younger brother Sheng.

sentences.zh: 太守趙興到，聞乃嘆曰：『我受漢茅土，不能立節，而鮑宣死之，豈可害其子也！

translations.literal: Governor Zhao Xing arrived—heard and sighed, saying: 'I received Han's fief and soil—could not establish integrity—while Bao Xuan died for it—how could I harm his sons!
translations.idiomatic: The new governor Zhao Xing said: "I hold Han land yet lacked Bao Xuan's courage—how could I harm his son?

sentences.zh: 』敕縣出升，復署永功曹。

translations.literal: ' Ordered the county to release Sheng—again appointed Yong Merit Assessor.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered Sheng freed and put Yong back in office as merit assessor.

sentences.zh: 時，有矯稱侍中止傳舍者，興欲謁之。

translations.literal: At the time there was one falsely claiming Attendant-in-ordinary stopping at relay inn—Xing wished to visit him.
translations.idiomatic: Someone impersonating an attendant-in-ordinary stopped at the post station; Zhao Xing meant to pay a call.

sentences.zh: 永疑其詐，諫不聽而出，興遂駕往，永乃拔佩刀截馬當匈，乃止，後數日，莽詔書果下捕矯稱者，永由是知名。

translations.literal: Yong suspected his fraud—Xing did not listen and went out—Xing thereupon drove off—Yong then drew his belt knife and cut the horse's chest strap—thereupon stopped—after several days—Mang's edict indeed arrived ordering arrest of the false claimant—Yong thereby became known.
translations.idiomatic: Yong doubted the impostor; Zhao Xing rode out anyway until Yong slashed the carriage harness with his sword; days later Wang Mang's warrant proved Yong right and made his name.

sentences.zh: 舉秀才，不應。

translations.literal: Recommended flowering talent—did not respond.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as "flowering talent," he declined.

sentences.zh: 更始二年征，再遷尚書僕射，行大將軍事，持節將兵，安集河東、并州、朔部，得自置偏裨，輒行軍法。

translations.literal: In Gengshi's second year summoned—twice promoted Masters of Writing Deputy Supervisor—acting Grand General affairs—holding tally commanding troops—pacifying and gathering Hedong, Bingzhou, northern frontier—allowed to appoint deputies at will—then carrying out military law.
translations.idiomatic: In Gengshi 2 he was summoned, rose to masters-of-writing vice-director, acted as grand general with tally and troops, pacified Hedong, Bingzhou, and the northern frontier, appointed subordinates, and enforced military law.

sentences.zh: 永至河東，因擊青犢，大破之，更始封為中陽侯。

translations.literal: Yong reached Hedong—thereupon struck the Green Calves—greatly shattered them—Gengshi enfeoffed him Marquis of Zhongyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Hedong he smashed the Green Calves band and Gengshi enfeoffed him as marquis of Zhongyang.

sentences.zh: 永雖為將率，而車服敝素，為道路所識。

translations.literal: Although Yong was a general commander—yet carriage and clothing worn and plain—was recognized on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Though a commander he rode in rough plain dress that everyone on the road recognized.

sentences.zh: 時赤眉害更始，三輔道絕。

translations.literal: At the time the Red Eyebrows slew Gengshi—three auxiliaries roads cut off.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows killed Gengshi and cut the roads to the capital region.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，遣諫議大夫儲大伯，持節征永詣行在所。

translations.literal: Guangwu ascended the throne—dispatched Remonstrance Grandee Chu Dabo—holding tally summoning Yong to the mobile headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent remonstrance grandee Chu Dabo with tally to summon Yong to court.

sentences.zh: 永疑不從，乃收系大伯，遣使馳至長安。

translations.literal: Yong suspected and did not follow—thereupon seized and bound Dabo—dispatched envoy galloping to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Yong hesitated, held Chu Dabo under arrest, and sent riders to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 既知更始已亡，乃發喪，出大伯等，封上將軍列侯印綬，悉罷兵，但幅巾與諸將及同心客百餘人詣河內。

translations.literal: When he learned Gengshi had already perished—thereupon conducted mourning—released Dabo and others—sealed and presented general-in-chief and marquis seals and ribbons—wholly dismissed troops—only headcloth with various generals and like-minded guests over a hundred persons proceeding to Henei.
translations.idiomatic: Learning Gengshi was dead he mourned, freed Dabo, returned seals, dismissed his army, and with a hundred followers in plain cloth went to Henei.

sentences.zh: 帝見永，問曰：『卿眾所在？

translations.literal: The emperor saw Yong—inquired, saying: 'Where are your masses?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: "Where are your troops?

sentences.zh: 』永離席叩頭曰：『臣事更始，不能令全，誠慚以其眾幸富貴，故悉罷之。

translations.literal: ' Yong left seat and knocked head, saying: 'Your servant served Gengshi—could not make him whole—truly ashamed to use those masses to seek wealth and honor—therefore wholly dismissed them.
translations.idiomatic: Yong kowtowed: "I served Gengshi and could not save him; I would be ashamed to trade those men for rank—so I sent them home.

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『卿言大！

translations.literal: ' The emperor said: 'Your words are great!
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu replied: "Noble words!

sentences.zh: 』而意不悅。

translations.literal: ' Yet intent was not pleased.
translations.idiomatic: But his face stayed cold.

sentences.zh: 時攻懷未拔，帝謂永曰：『我攻懷三日而兵不下，關東畏服禦，可且將故人自往城下譬之。

translations.literal: At the time attacking Huai not yet taken—the emperor said to Yong: 'I attack Huai three days yet troops do not take it—the east fears and submits to your prestige—may for now lead old friends personally to the city wall to explain.
translations.idiomatic: Huai still held out after three days; Guangwu said those east of the pass feared Yong's reputation—he should take old allies below the walls to persuade the city.

sentences.zh: 』即拜永諫議大夫。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon appointed Yong Remonstrance Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: He named Yong remonstrance grandee on the spot.

sentences.zh: 至懷，乃說更始河內太守，於是開城而降。

translations.literal: Reaching Huai—thereupon persuaded Gengshi's Henei governor—thereupon opened city and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: At Huai he talked Gengshi's governor of Henei into opening the gates.

sentences.zh: 帝大喜，賜永洛陽商裏宅，固辭不受。

translations.literal: The emperor greatly rejoiced—bestowed on Yong a mansion in Luoyang's Shang ward—firmly declined and did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: Delighted, Guangwu offered a mansion in Luoyang's Shang quarter; Yong firmly refused.

sentences.zh: 時，董憲裨將屯兵於魯，侵害百姓，乃拜永為魯郡太守。

translations.literal: At the time Dong Xian's deputy general stationed troops at Lu—encroaching and harming common people—thereupon appointed Yong Governor of Lu command.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian's lieutenant was ravaging Lu; Yong was named governor there.

sentences.zh: 永到，擊討，大破之，降者數千人。

translations.literal: Yong arrived—struck and campaigned—greatly shattered them—those who surrendered numbered several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: He attacked, routed the enemy, and accepted thousands of surrenders.

sentences.zh: 唯別帥彭豐、虞休、皮常等各千餘人，稱『將軍』，不脹下。

translations.literal: Only separate commanders Peng Feng, Yu Xiu, Pi Chang and others each over a thousand persons—called themselves 'general'—would not submit.
translations.idiomatic: Splinter leaders Peng Feng, Yu Xiu, Pi Chang and others—over a thousand each—styled themselves generals and refused to yield.

sentences.zh: 頃之，孔子闕里無故荊棘自除，從講堂至於裏門。

translations.literal: Shortly—Confucius' Que village without cause brambles cleared themselves—from lecture hall reaching to ward gate.
translations.idiomatic: Soon thorns at Confucius' hometown cleared themselves from the lecture hall to the lane gate.

sentences.zh: 永異之，謂府丞及魯令曰：『方今危急而闕里自開，斯豈夫子欲令太守行禮，助吾誅無道邪？

translations.literal: Yong wondered at it—said to administrative assistant and Lu magistrate: 'Now critically urgent yet Que village opens itself—is this not the Master wishing to order the governor to perform ritual—help me execute the lawless?
translations.idiomatic: Yong told his aides: "The sage's lane opens in crisis—is the Master bidding me perform the village archery rite and help purge the wicked?

sentences.zh: 』乃會人眾，修鄉射之禮，請豐等共會觀視，欲因此禽之。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon assembled people masses—repaired village archery ritual—invited Feng and others jointly to meet and observe—wishing thereby to seize them.
translations.idiomatic: He gathered the people for the archery ceremony and invited Peng Feng to watch—planning to seize him there.

sentences.zh: 豐等亦欲圖永，乃持牛酒勞饗，而潛挾兵器。

translations.literal: Feng and others also wished to plot Yong—thereupon holding ox and wine feasted and entertained—yet secretly carried arms.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Feng meant to trap Yong too and came with ox and wine while hiding weapons.

sentences.zh: 永覺之，手格殺豐等，禽破黨與。

translations.literal: Yong perceived it—personally grappled and killed Feng and others—captured and shattered party associates.
translations.idiomatic: Yong saw through it, slew Peng Feng in hand combat, and broke up the band.

sentences.zh: 帝嘉其略，封為關內侯，遷楊州牧。

translations.literal: The emperor praised his strategy—enfeoffed him Marquis Within the Pass—promoted him Governor of Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved his plan, enfeoffed him as marquis within the passes, and named him governor of Yangzhou.

sentences.zh: 時南土尚多寇暴，永以吏人痍傷之後，乃緩其銜轡，示誅強橫而鎮撫其餘，百姓安之。

translations.literal: At the time the south still had many bandit raids—Yong because clerks and people were wounded after—then loosened their bridles—showed executing the strong and overbearing and pacified the rest—the hundred surnames were settled.
translations.idiomatic: The south was still plagued by raiders; Yong eased the reins after war had wounded officials and commoners alike—executing the violent few while reassuring everyone else until the people rested easy.

sentences.zh: 會遭母憂，去官，悉以財產與孤弟子。

translations.literal: Meeting mother's mourning—left office—wholly gave property to orphaned nephew.
translations.idiomatic: When his mother died he resigned and handed his entire estate to a bereaved nephew.

sentences.zh: 建武十一年，征為司隸校尉。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year of Jianwu—summoned as Metropolitan Superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 11 he was summoned to serve as metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 帝叔父趙王良尊戚貴重，永以事劾良大不敬，由是朝廷肅然，莫不戒慎。

translations.literal: The emperor's uncle Prince Zhao Liang—noble kin honored and weighty—Yong by affair impeached Liang for grave disrespect—thereby the court became solemn—none did not guard cautiously.
translations.idiomatic: When Yong impeached the emperor's uncle, Prince Zhao Liang of Zhao, for grave disrespect, the whole court sat up straight—no one dared take liberties.

sentences.zh: 乃辟扶風鮑恢為都官從事，恢亦抗直不避強禦。

translations.literal: Thereupon he recruited Bao Hui of Fufeng as Capital Clerk Investigator—Hui also was upright and straight—not avoiding strong opposition.
translations.idiomatic: He employed Bao Hui of Fufeng as metropolitan clerk-investigator—another man as blunt as Yong who did not flinch from the powerful.

sentences.zh: 帝常曰：『貴戚且宜斂手，以避二鮑。

translations.literal: The emperor constantly said: 'Noble kin ought for now draw in hands—to avoid the two Baos.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu warned his kin: "Keep your hands clean and stay clear of the two Baos.

sentences.zh: 』其見憚如此。

translations.literal: ' Thus he was feared thus.
translations.idiomatic: Such was the fear he inspired.

sentences.zh: 永行縣到霸陵，路經更始墓，引車入陌，從事諫止之。

translations.literal: Yong traveling counties reached Baling—road passed Gengshi's tomb—drew carriage into side path—clerk-investigator remonstrated stopping him.
translations.idiomatic: Inspecting Baling he passed Gengshi's tomb, turned onto a lane toward it; his aide urged him not to.

sentences.zh: 永曰：『親北面事人，寧有過墓不拜！

translations.literal: Yong said: 'Personally north-facing serving man—how can passing tomb not bow!
translations.idiomatic: Yong said: "I once faced north as his subject—how pass his grave without bowing?

sentences.zh: 雖以獲罪，司隸所不避也。

translations.literal: Although thereby incurring guilt—what Metropolitan Superintendent avoids?
translations.idiomatic: Even if it costs me—there are duties a metropolitan superintendent does not dodge.

sentences.zh: 』遂下拜，哭盡哀而去。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon descended bowed—cried utmost grief and left.
translations.idiomatic: He dismounted, bowed, wept his fill, and rode on.

sentences.zh: 西至扶風，椎牛上茍諫冢。

translations.literal: West reaching Fufeng—beat ox atop Gou Jian's tomb.
translations.idiomatic: West of the capital he slaughtered an ox on Governor Gou Jian's grave.

sentences.zh: 帝聞之，意不平，問公卿曰：『奉使如此何如？

translations.literal: The emperor heard—intent not level—inquired of dukes and ministers: 'Serving thus—how is it?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu heard and bristled; he asked his ministers what he thought of such behavior.

sentences.zh: 』太中大夫張湛對曰：『仁者行之宗，忠者義之主也。

translations.literal: ' Grand Palace Grandee Zhang Zhan replied: 'Benevolence is conduct's ancestor—loyalty is righteousness's lord.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Zhan answered: "Humaneness is the root of conduct; loyalty is the master of duty.

sentences.zh: 仁不遺舊，忠不忘君，行之高者也。

translations.literal: Benevolence does not abandon old—loyalty does not forget lord—conduct's height.
translations.idiomatic: The humane do not cast off old ties; the loyal do not forget their lord—that is the summit of conduct.

sentences.zh: 』帝意乃釋。

translations.literal: ' The emperor's intent then released.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's anger cooled.

sentences.zh: 後大司徒韓歆坐事，永固請之不得，以此忤帝意，出為東海相。

translations.literal: Later Grand Minister of Education Han Xin sat for affair—Yong firmly pleaded for him—not obtaining—thereby disobeyed emperor's intent—sent out as Chancellor of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: When Grand Minister Han Xin fell foul of the court Yong pleaded for him in vain and offended Guangwu, who sent him out as chancellor of Donghai.

sentences.zh: 坐度田事不實，被征，諸郡守多下獄。

translations.literal: Sitting for land survey affair untrue—was summoned—many commandery governors cast into prison.
translations.idiomatic: He was cited for false reports on the land survey; many governors landed in jail with him.

sentences.zh: 永至成臯，詔書逆拜為兗州牧，便道之官。

translations.literal: Yong reached Chenggao—edict contrary appointed him Yanzhou Governor—by convenient road proceeded to office.
translations.idiomatic: At Chenggao an edict met him and named him governor of Yanzhou; he took the direct route to his post.

sentences.zh: 視事三年，病卒。

translations.literal: Handling affairs three years—sick—died.
translations.idiomatic: After three years in office he fell ill and died.

sentences.zh: 子昱。

translations.literal: Son Yu.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Bao Yu.

sentences.zh: 論曰：鮑永守義於故主，斯可以事新主矣。

translations.literal: The judgment says: Bao Yong kept righteousness toward former lord—this can serve new lord.
translations.idiomatic: The historian concludes: Yong kept faith with his old master—so he could honor the new.

sentences.zh: 恥以其眾受寵，斯可以受大寵矣。

translations.literal: Ashamed to use those masses to receive favor—this can receive great favor.
translations.idiomatic: Ashamed to trade his troops for favor—he earned the greater favor.

sentences.zh: 若乃言之者雖誠，而聞之未譬，豈茍進之悅，易以情納，持正之忤，難以理求乎？

translations.literal: If then those who speak are indeed sincere—yet those who hear have not understood—is it that careless advancement's pleasure—easily admitted by feeling—upholding straightness's disobedience—hard to seek by principle?
translations.idiomatic: When advice is honest yet fails to register, is it because flattery slips in through feeling while plain truth cannot win a hearing on reason alone?

sentences.zh: 誠能釋利以循道，居方以從義，君子之概也。

translations.literal: Truly able to release profit to follow the Way—dwell squareness to follow righteousness—gentleman's bearing.
translations.idiomatic: To renounce gain for the Way and hold square to righteousness—that is a gentleman's measure.

sentences.zh: 昱字文泉。

translations.literal: Yu's style was Wenquan.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Yu, courtesy name Wenquan.

sentences.zh: 少傳父學，客授於東平。

translations.literal: Youth transmitted father's learning—guest-taught at Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: In youth he carried on his father's scholarship and taught abroad in Dongping.

sentences.zh: 建武初，太行山中有劇賊，太守戴涉聞昱鮑永子，有智略，乃就謁，請署守高都長，昱應之，遂討擊群賊，誅其渠帥，道路開通，由是知名。

translations.literal: At Jianwu's beginning—inside Taihang mountains were fierce bandits—Governor Dai She heard Yu was Bao Yong's son—had wisdom and strategy—thereupon visited—requested appoint acting Magistrate of Gaodu—Yu responded—thereupon campaigned and struck bandit hosts—executed their chiefs—roads opened—thereby became known.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Jianwu, Taihang bandits ran riot; Governor Dai She, hearing that Bao Yu was Yong's talented son, called on him and made him acting magistrate of Gaodu. Yu crushed the bands, slew their leaders, and opened the roads—making his name.

sentences.zh: 後為沘陽長，政化仁愛，境內清凈。

translations.literal: Later became Biyang magistrate—government-transforming benevolent love—within borders clear and pure.
translations.idiomatic: Later as magistrate of Biyang he ruled with kindness and kept the district calm.

sentences.zh: 荊州刺史表上之，再遷，中元元年，拜司隸校尉，詔昱詣尚書，使封胡降檄。

translations.literal: Jingzhou Inspector memorialized upward—twice promoted—first year of Zhongyuan—appointed Metropolitan Superintendent—edict Yu proceed to Masters of Writing—ordered seal barbarian surrender proclamation.
translations.idiomatic: The Jingzhou inspector recommended him; after two promotions, in Zhongyuan 1 he became metropolitan superintendent. The emperor summoned him to the Masters of Writing to seal a proclamation to surrendering barbarians.

sentences.zh: 光武遣小黃門問昱有所怪不？

translations.literal: Guangwu dispatched Junior Yellow Gate to ask Yu whether anything was strange or not?
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent a junior Yellow Gate eunuch to ask whether anything struck Yu as odd.

sentences.zh: 對曰：『臣聞故事通官文書不著姓，又當司徒露布，怪使司隸下書而著姓也。

translations.literal: Replied, saying: 'Your servant has heard precedent—passing official documents do not show surname—also ought Grand Minister of Education expose edict—strange that Metropolitan Superintendent's lower document shows surname.
translations.idiomatic: Yu replied: "By precedent routine documents omit surnames; edicts for the Grand Minister of Education go bare—yet my commission bears my surname.

sentences.zh: 』帝報曰：『吾故欲今天下知忠臣之子復為司隸也。

translations.literal: ' The emperor replied, saying: 'I intentionally wished to make all under Heaven know loyal minister's son again became Metropolitan Superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered: "I meant the empire to see a loyal minister's son serve again as metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 』昱在職，奉法守正，有父風。

translations.literal: ' Yu in office—upheld law guarded rectitude—had father's manner.
translations.idiomatic: Yu enforced the law like his father.

sentences.zh: 永平五年，坐救火遲，免。

translations.literal: In fifth year of Yongping—sat for slow fire rescue—dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 5 he was dismissed for slow response to a fire.

sentences.zh: 後拜汝南太守。

translations.literal: Later appointed Governor of Runan.
translations.idiomatic: He was later named governor of Runan.

sentences.zh: 郡多陂池，歲歲決壞，年費常三千餘萬。

translations.literal: The commandery had many ponds and pools—yearly bursting banks—annual expense constantly over thirty million.
translations.idiomatic: The commandery abounded in dikes and reservoirs that burst each year at a cost of thirty million cash or more.

sentences.zh: 昱乃上作方梁石洫，水常饒足，溉田倍多，人以殷富。

translations.literal: Yu thereupon upward built square beam stone canal—water constantly abundant—irrigated fields doubled—people thereby prospered.
translations.idiomatic: Yu built stone sluices and aqueducts so water stayed plentiful; irrigated acreage doubled and the people grew rich.

sentences.zh: 四年，代牟融為太尉，六年，薨，年七十餘。

translations.literal: Fourth year—replaced Mou Rong as Grand Commandant—sixth year—passed away—aged over seventy.
translations.idiomatic: In year 4 he succeeded Mou Rong as grand commandant; he died in year 6 in his seventies.

sentences.zh: 子德，修誌節，有名稱，累官為南陽太守。

translations.literal: Son De—cultivated intent integrity—had reputation—accumulated offices became Governor of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: His son Bao De cultivated principle and rose to governor of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 時歲多荒災，唯南陽豐穰。

translations.literal: At the time years many wasteland disasters—only Nanyang abundant harvest.
translations.idiomatic: Years of famine stalked the empire—only Nanyang had full granaries.

sentences.zh: 吏人愛悅，號為神父。

translations.literal: Clerks and people loved and delighted—styled him Father Spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners loved him and called him "Father Spirit."

sentences.zh: 時郡學久廢，德乃修起橫舍，備俎豆黻冕，行禮奏樂。

translations.literal: At the time commandery school long wasted—De then repaired and raised horizontal lodges—prepared sacrificial vessels and robes—performed ritual played music.
translations.idiomatic: The county school had long lain idle; De rebuilt the halls, set out vessels and vestments, and held rites with music.

sentences.zh: 又尊饗國老，宴會諸儒。

translations.literal: Further honored and feasted state elders—banqueted various Ru scholars.
translations.idiomatic: He gave place of honor to village elders and feasted local scholars.

sentences.zh: 百姓觀者，莫不勸服。

translations.literal: Common people who watched—none not urged and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Every spectator went home persuaded.

sentences.zh: 在職九年，征拜大司農，卒於官。

translations.literal: In office nine years—summoned appointed Grand Minister of Agriculture—died in office.
translations.idiomatic: After nine years he was recalled as grand minister of agriculture and died in office.

sentences.zh: 子昂，字叔雅，有孝義節行。

translations.literal: Son Ang—style Shuya—had filial righteousness integrity conduct.
translations.idiomatic: His grandson Bao Ang, courtesy name Shuya, was famed for filial duty and integrity.

sentences.zh: 初，德被病數年，昂俯伏左右，衣不緩帶；

translations.literal: First—De afflicted ill several years—Ang bent prostrate left and right—clothes not loosen belt;
translations.idiomatic: When De lay ill for years, Ang stayed at his bedside without loosening his belt;

sentences.zh: 及處喪，毀瘠三年，抱負乃行；

translations.literal: When handling mourning—wasted lean three years—embraced burden then walked;
translations.idiomatic: in mourning he wasted away for three years and had to be carried;

sentences.zh: 服闋，遂潛於墓次，不關時務。

translations.literal: Mourning completed—thereupon hid at tomb side—not attending temporal affairs.
translations.idiomatic: when the rites ended he stayed by the tomb and shunned public life.

sentences.zh: 舉孝廉，辟公府，連征不至，卒於家。

translations.literal: Recommended Filial and Incorrupt—summoned to duke's mansion—repeatedly summoned did not arrive—died at home.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and honest and called to high office, he never answered and died at home.

sentences.zh: 郅惲字君章，汝南西平人也。

translations.literal: Zhi Yun's style was Junzhang—a man of Xiping in Runan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Yun, courtesy name Junzhang, came from Xiping in Runan.

sentences.zh: 年十二失母，居喪過禮。

translations.literal: Age twelve lost mother—dwelling mourning exceeded ritual.
translations.idiomatic: He lost his mother at twelve and mourned beyond the prescribed rites.

sentences.zh: 及長，理《韓詩》、《嚴氏春秋》，明天文歷數。

translations.literal: When grown—studied Han's Odes and Yan's Spring and Autumn—understood astronomy and calendrical reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: As a man he mastered the Han school of the Odes and Yan's Spring and Autumn Annals and understood astronomy and numerology.

sentences.zh: 王莽時，寇賊群發，惲乃仰占玄象，嘆謂友人曰：『方今鎮、歲、熒惑並在漢分翼、軫之域，去而復來，漢必再受命，福歸有德。

translations.literal: At Wang Mang's time—bandits swarmed forth—Yun then upward observed dark patterns—sighed telling friends, saying: 'Now Jupiter, Saturn, Mars together in Han's division Wings and Chariot's region—departed yet again come—Han surely again receives mandate—blessing returns to the virtuous.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang, Yun read the stars and told friends: "Jupiter, Saturn, and Mars stand together in the Wings and Chariot—the Han asterism; they left and returned—Han will receive the mandate again and fortune will favor the worthy.

sentences.zh: 如有順天發策者，必成大功。

translations.literal: If there is one who obeys Heaven and raises strategy—must complete great merit.
translations.idiomatic: Whoever answers Heaven's call will win a great achievement.

sentences.zh: 』時左隊大夫逯並素好士，惲說之曰：『當今上天垂象，智者以昌，愚者以亡。

translations.literal: ' At the time Left Corps Grandee Lu Bing always loved gentlemen—Yun persuaded him, saying: 'Now upper Heaven hangs signs—wise ones thereby flourish—foolish ones thereby perish.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Bing of the Left Corps loved talent; Yun urged him: "Heaven is signing—wise men rise and fools fall.

sentences.zh: 昔伊尹自鬻輔商，立功全人。

translations.literal: Formerly Yi Yin sold himself aiding Shang—completed merit preserved people.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Yin once sold himself to aid Shang and saved the people.

sentences.zh: 惲竊不遜，敢希伊尹之蹤，應天人之變。

translations.literal: Yun rudely presumes not humble—dares hope Yi Yin's tracks—respond Heaven and man's changes.
translations.idiomatic: I dare aspire to Yi Yin's path and answer Heaven and humanity.

sentences.zh: 明府儻不疑逆，俾成天德。

translations.literal: Illustrious prefect if not doubting rebellion—will complete Heaven's virtue.
translations.idiomatic: If you do not doubt omens of revolt, you will fulfill Heaven's power.

sentences.zh: 』並奇之，使署為吏。

translations.literal: ' Bing marveled at him—ordered appoint as clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Bing marveled and offered him a clerkship.

sentences.zh: 惲不謁，曰：『昔文王拔呂尚於渭濱，高宗禮傅說於巖築，桓公取管仲於射鉤，故能立弘烈，就元勛。

translations.literal: Yun did not pay court visit—said: 'Formerly King Wen pulled Lü Shang from Wei's bank—Gaozong ritually Fu Yue from cliff building—Duke Huan took Guan Zhong from shooting hook—therefore able to establish vast merit—complete primal merit.
translations.idiomatic: Yun declined: "Wen met Taigong by the Wei; Wuding hailed Fu Yue from the cliffs; Huan Gong took Guan Zhong from captivity—never were chief ministers hired as mere clerks.

sentences.zh: 未聞師相仲父，而可為吏位也。

translations.literal: Not heard teacher-minister uncle-like—yet can be clerk post.
translations.idiomatic: No one ever treated a chief counselor like a petty clerk.

sentences.zh: 非窺天者不可與圖遠。

translations.literal: Not one who peers into Heaven—may not with plan far.
translations.idiomatic: Only one who reads Heaven may plan for the long term.

sentences.zh: 君不授驥以重任，驥亦俯首裹足而去耳。

translations.literal: If lord does not give thoroughbred heavy burden—thoroughbred also lowers head wraps hooves and leaves.
translations.idiomatic: If you will not give the stallion a great burden, it will lower its head and walk away.

sentences.zh: 』遂不受署。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon did not accept appointment.
translations.idiomatic: He refused the post.

sentences.zh: 西至長安，乃上書王莽曰：

translations.literal: West reaching Chang'an—thereupon upward memorialized Wang Mang, saying:
translations.idiomatic: In Chang'an he submitted a memorial to Wang Mang:

sentences.zh: 臣聞天地重其人，惜其物，故運機衡，垂日月，含元包一，甄陶品類，顯表紀世，圖錄豫設。

translations.literal: Your minister has heard Heaven and Earth value their persons—cherish their things—therefore operate pivot and steelyard—hang sun and moon—embrace primordial wrap unity—mold and fire myriad kinds—manifest mark record ages—charts and registers beforehand set.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant has heard that Heaven and Earth treasure mankind and their creatures; they set the Dipper, raise sun and moon, fashion all things, and ordain the succession long before it arrives.

sentences.zh: 漢歷久長，孔為赤制，不使愚惑，殘人亂時。

translations.literal: Han's calendar long—Kong established red institutions—not letting folly confuse—injure people disorder times.
translations.idiomatic: Han's span was fixed in Confucius' crimson charter so that fools would not misrule the age.

sentences.zh: 智者順以成德，愚者逆以取害，神器有命，不可虛獲。

translations.literal: Wise ones follow thereby complete virtue—foolish ones oppose thereby take harm—divine vessel has mandate—cannot emptily obtain.
translations.idiomatic: The wise succeed by following Heaven; the foolish perish by opposing it—the throne has its destiny and cannot be seized in vain.

sentences.zh: 上天垂戒，欲悟陛下，令就臣位，轉禍為福。

translations.literal: Upper Heaven hangs warning—wishes awaken Your Majesty—commanding yield minister position—turn misfortune into blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven warns you to step down from usurped rank and turn doom into blessing.

sentences.zh: 劉氏享天永命，陛下順節盛衰，取之以天，還之以天，可謂知命矣。

translations.literal: Liu clan enjoys Heaven's long mandate—Your Majesty follows season's flourishing decline—takes it by Heaven—returns it by Heaven—can be called knowing mandate.
translations.idiomatic: The house of Liu holds Heaven's long decree; you took power when fate waned—take it from Heaven and return it to Heaven and you may be called wise to destiny.

sentences.zh: 若不早圖，是不免於竊位也。

translations.literal: If not early plotting—this is not avoiding stealing position.
translations.idiomatic: Refuse to plan now and you steal the throne beyond recall.

sentences.zh: 且堯、舜不以天顯自與，故禪天下，陛下何貪非天顯以自累也？

translations.literal: Moreover Yao and Shun did not by Heaven's sign give self—therefore ceded all under Heaven—Your Majesty why greed not Heaven's sign to self-tire?
translations.idiomatic: Yao and Shun did not monopolize celestial omens but yielded the realm—why cling to a stolen sign and ruin yourself?

sentences.zh: 天為陛下嚴父，臣為陛下孝子。

translations.literal: Heaven is Your Majesty's stern father—your minister is Your Majesty's filial son.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven is your stern father; your servant is your dutiful son.

sentences.zh: 父教不可廢，子諫不可拒，惟陛下留神。

translations.literal: Father's teaching cannot be abolished—son's remonstrance cannot be rejected—only Your Majesty retain spirit.
translations.idiomatic: A father's lesson stands; a son's warning must be heard—think on this.

sentences.zh: 莽大怒，即收系詔獄，劾以大逆。

translations.literal: Mang greatly angry—immediately seized and bound in edict prison—impeached with great rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang flew into a rage, jailed him under the edict prison, and charged him with high treason.

sentences.zh: 猶以惲據經讖，難即害之，使黃門近臣脅惲，令自告狂病恍忽，不覺所言。

translations.literal: Still because Yun relied on classics and prophecies—hard immediately harm him—ordered Yellow Gate near ministers threaten Yun—command self-report mad sickness dim—not aware what was spoken.
translations.idiomatic: Because Yun cited omens, Mang hesitated to kill him and had eunuchs pressure him to plead madness.

sentences.zh: 惲乃瞋目詈曰：『所陳皆天文聖意，非狂人所能造。

translations.literal: Yun then glared eyes and cursed, saying: 'What was stated are astronomy's sage intentions—not what mad men can craft.
translations.idiomatic: Yun glared and swore: "Every word came from Heaven's pattern—not mad ravings.

sentences.zh: 』遂系須冬，會赦得出，乃與同郡鄭敬南遁蒼梧。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon bound awaited winter—met amnesty obtained release—thereupon with same commandery Zheng Jing south fled Cangwu.
translations.idiomatic: He was held until winter; after an amnesty he fled south with Zheng Jing of his district to Cangwu.

sentences.zh: 建武三年，又至廬江，因遇積弩將軍傅俊東徇揚州。

translations.literal: In third year of Jianwu—again reached Lujiang—thereupon met Massed Crossbow General Fu Jun east campaigning Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 3 he was in Lujiang when Massed-Crossbow General Fu Jun campaigned east through Yangzhou.

sentences.zh: 俊素聞惲名，乃禮請之，上為將兵長史，授以軍政。

translations.literal: Jun always heard Yun's name—thereupon ritually invited him—upward made Troop Leader clerk—entrusted military government.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Jun, who had long heard of Yun, treated him with ceremony and had him named senior clerk with military authority.

sentences.zh: 惲乃誓眾曰：『無掩人不備，窮人於厄，不得斷人支體，裸人形骸，放淫婦女。

translations.literal: Yun thereupon swore masses, saying: 'Do not ambush others unprepared—drive others to straits—must not sever people's limbs—strip people's skeletons—release wanton women.
translations.idiomatic: Yun harangued the troops: "Strike no unprepared foe; drive no foe to hopeless straits; mutilate no captives, strip no corpses, molest no women.

sentences.zh: 』俊軍士猶發冢陳屍，掠奪百姓。

translations.literal: ' Jun's soldiers still dug tombs exposed corpses—plundered common people.
translations.idiomatic: Jun's men still looted tombs and ravaged the people.

sentences.zh: 惲諫俊曰：『昔文王不忍露白骨，武王不以天下易一人之命，故能獲天地之應，克商如林之旅。

translations.literal: Yun remonstrated Jun, saying: 'Formerly King Wen could not bear expose white bones—King Wu did not trade all under Heaven for one person's life—therefore able to obtain Heaven and Earth's response—conquer Shang like forest's hosts.
translations.idiomatic: Yun urged Fu Jun: "King Wen would not leave bones bare; King Wu would not trade the realm for one life—thus they won Heaven and shattered Shang like chaff.

sentences.zh: 將軍如何不師法文王，而犯逆天地之禁，多傷人害物，虐及枯屍，取罪神明？

translations.literal: General how not model King Wen—yet violate oppose Heaven and Earth's prohibitions—much injure people harm things—cruelty reaching withered corpses—take guilt from spirits bright?
translations.idiomatic: Why not imitate Wen yet break Heaven's law, harm the living and dead, and offend the gods?

sentences.zh: 今不謝天改政，無以全命。

translations.literal: Now not thanking Heaven reforming government—cannot thereby preserve life.
translations.idiomatic: Unless you repent and change course you cannot save your mandate.

sentences.zh: 願將軍親率士卒，收傷葬死，哭所殘暴，以明非將軍本意也。

translations.literal: Wish general personally lead soldiers—collect wounded bury dead—weep for brutalized places—to clarify not general's original intent.
translations.idiomatic: Lead the troops yourself: bury the dead, mourn the wronged, and show the world this was not your wish.

sentences.zh: 』從之，百姓悅服，所向皆下。

translations.literal: ' Jun followed—common people pleased and submitted—where aimed all fell.
translations.idiomatic: Jun obeyed; the people rejoiced and every city submitted.

sentences.zh: 七年，俊還京師，而上論之。

translations.literal: Seventh year—Jun returned capital—and upward discussed him.
translations.idiomatic: In year 7 Fu Jun returned to the capital and praised him to the throne.

sentences.zh: 惲恥以軍功取位，遂辭歸鄉裏。

translations.literal: Yun ashamed to use military merit take position—thereupon resigned returned village.
translations.idiomatic: Ashamed to gain office by mere battlefield credit, Yun went home.

sentences.zh: 縣令卑身崇禮，請以為門下掾。

translations.literal: County magistrate lowered body honored ritual—requested take him as gate clerk.
translations.idiomatic: The county magistrate humbled himself and offered Yun a post as clerk.

sentences.zh: 惲友人董子張者，父先為鄉人所害。

translations.literal: Yun's friend Dong Zizhang—father before harmed by village people.
translations.idiomatic: His friend Dong Zizhang's father had been murdered by townsfolk.

sentences.zh: 及子張病，將終，惲往候之。

translations.literal: When Zizhang sick—about to end—Yun went visit him.
translations.idiomatic: When Zizhang lay dying Yun visited him.

sentences.zh: 子張垂歿，視惲，歔欷不能言。

translations.literal: Zizhang hanging perish—looked Yun—sobbed unable speak.
translations.idiomatic: Zizhang, near death, could only stare at Yun and weep.

sentences.zh: 惲曰：『吾知子不悲天命，而痛仇不復也。

translations.literal: Yun said: 'Your servant knows you do not grieve Heaven's mandate—but pain revenge not restored.
translations.idiomatic: Yun said: "You do not fear death—you ache that your father is unavenged.

sentences.zh: 子在，吾憂而不手；

translations.literal: While you alive—your servant worried but not act hand;
translations.idiomatic: While you lived I held back from striking in your stead;

sentences.zh: 子亡，吾手而不憂也。

translations.literal: You dead—your servant acts hand but not worried.
translations.idiomatic: Now that you are gone I will kill without regret.

sentences.zh: 』子張但目擊而已。

translations.literal: ' Zizhang only eye struck.
translations.idiomatic: Zizhang answered only with his eyes.

sentences.zh: 惲即起，將客遮仇人，取其頭以示子張。

translations.literal: Yun immediately rose—led retainers to intercept the foe—took his head to show Zizhang.
translations.idiomatic: Yun rose at once, led his men to cut off his enemy, and brought the head back for Zizhang to see.

sentences.zh: 子張見而氣絕。

translations.literal: Zizhang saw—and breath stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Zizhang looked—and died.

sentences.zh: 惲因而詣縣，以狀自首。

translations.literal: Yun thereupon proceeded to county—with petition self-surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: He went straight to the magistrate and gave himself up.

sentences.zh: 令應之遲，惲曰：『為友報仇，吏之私也。

translations.literal: The magistrate responded slowly—Yun said: 'Avenging a friend—a clerk's private affair.
translations.idiomatic: When the magistrate hesitated, Yun said: "Blood revenge for a friend is my private duty as your clerk.

sentences.zh: 奉法不阿，君之義也。

translations.literal: Upholding law not bending—lord's righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Serving the law without favor—that is your honor as magistrate.

sentences.zh: 虧君以生，非臣節也。

translations.literal: Harming lord to live—not minister's integrity.
translations.idiomatic: I cannot save myself at your expense—that would violate every duty I owe you.

sentences.zh: 』趨出就獄。

translations.literal: ' Hurried out into prison.
translations.idiomatic: He strode to the jail and locked himself in.

sentences.zh: 令跣而追惲，不及，遂自至獄，令拔刃自向以要惲曰：『子不從我出，敢以死明心。

translations.literal: Magistrate bare-footed pursued Yun—did not catch—thereupon self reached prison—magistrate drew blade self-faced to demand Yun, saying: 'If you do not follow me out—dare use death to clarify mind.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate ran after him barefoot, failed to overtake, followed him into the cell, and set a blade to his own breast: "Leave with me or I die to prove my heart.

sentences.zh: 』惲得此乃出，因病去。

translations.literal: ' Yun obtained this then came out—because of illness left.
translations.idiomatic: Yun relented; he later resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 久之，太守歐陽歙請為功曹。

translations.literal: After long time—Governor Ouyang She requested him as Merit Assessor.
translations.idiomatic: Later Governor Ouyang She named him merit assessor.

sentences.zh: 汝南舊俗，十月饗會，百里內縣皆賫牛酒到府宴飲。

translations.literal: Runan old custom—tenth month feast meeting—within hundred li counties all brought oxen and wine to commandery to banquet drink.
translations.idiomatic: By old Runan custom, in the tenth month every county within a hundred li hauled beef and wine to the commandery for a great feast.

sentences.zh: 時臨饗禮訖，歙教曰：『西部督郵繇延，天資忠貞，稟性公方，摧破奸兇，不嚴而理。

translations.literal: At the time approaching feast rite completed—She instructed, saying: 'Western Supervising Clerk Yao Yan—Heaven-given nature loyal and firm—endowed nature public and square—shattered treacherous wicked—without severity yet ordered.
translations.idiomatic: When the rite ended, She announced: "Our western clerk Yao Yan is loyal, just, and has broken the bandits without harshness.

sentences.zh: 今與眾儒共論延功，顯之於朝。

translations.literal: Now with various Ru together discuss Yan's merit—display it at court.
translations.idiomatic: I ask the scholars to praise his deeds and make them known at court.

sentences.zh: 太守敬嘉厥休，牛酒養德。

translations.literal: The governor respectfully honors his excellence—oxen and wine nurture virtue.
translations.idiomatic: I honor his goodness with this beef and wine.

sentences.zh: 』主簿讀教，戶曹引延受賜。

translations.literal: ' Chief clerk read instruction—Household Clerk led Yan to receive gift.
translations.idiomatic: The chief clerk read the order; the household clerk led Yan forward to accept the gift.

sentences.zh: 惲於下坐愀然前曰：『司正舉觥，以君之罪，告謝於天。

translations.literal: Yun from lower seat somberly advanced, saying: 'The toastmaster raises the goblet—by your lordship's fault—declares apology to Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: From the lowest seat Yun stepped forward: "The toastmaster lifts the cup against your offense before Heaven.

sentences.zh: 案延資性貪邪，外方內員，朋黨構奸，罔上害人，所在荒亂，怨慝並作。

translations.literal: Investigate Yan's nature greedy and wicked—outside square inside round—faction-party plots treachery—deceives above harms people—where he went wasteland and chaos—resentment and evil together arose.
translations.idiomatic: Yan is greedy and crooked: square in face, round in theft; he forms cliques, cheats his superiors, and leaves turmoil and hatred wherever he serves.

sentences.zh: 明府以惡為善，股肱以直從曲，此既無君，又復無臣，惲敢再拜奉觥。

translations.literal: Illustrious prefect takes evil for good—thigh and arm follows crooked—this already lacks lord—again lacks minister—Yun dares twice bow and present goblet.
translations.idiomatic: When you call such a man good and your aides bow to wrong, there is neither true ruler nor true minister—I offer this cup of reproof.

sentences.zh: 』歙色慚動，不知所言。

translations.literal: ' She's color ashamed moved—did not know what to say.
translations.idiomatic: She flushed and could not answer.

sentences.zh: 門下掾鄭敬進曰：『君明臣直，功曹言切，明府德也。

translations.literal: Gate clerk Zheng Jing advanced, saying: 'Lord bright minister straight—Merit Assessor's words cutting—illustrious prefect's virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Clerk Zheng Jing said: "A wise governor welcomes blunt counsel—that proves your virtue.

sentences.zh: 可無受觥哉？

translations.literal: Can there be not accepting goblet?
translations.idiomatic: Will you refuse the cup?

sentences.zh: 』歙意少解，曰：『實歙罪也，敬奉觥。

translations.literal: ' She's intent slightly eased—said: 'Truly She's fault—respectfully receive goblet.
translations.idiomatic: She said: "The fault is mine—I drink.

sentences.zh: 』惲乃免寇謝曰：『昔虞舜輔堯，四罪鹹服，讒言弗庸，孔任不行，故能作股肱，帝用有歌。

translations.literal: ' Yun then removed cap thanked, saying: 'Formerly Yu Shun aided Yao—four crimes all submitted—slanderous words not employed—evil appointment not practiced—therefore able to be thigh and arm—emperor used had song.
translations.idiomatic: Yun bowed: "When Shun aided Yao the Four Evils fell; slander failed and bad appointments ceased—then ministers became true limbs and the emperor had cause to sing.

sentences.zh: 惲不忠，孔任是昭，豺虎從政，既陷誹謗，又露所言，罪莫重焉。

translations.literal: Yun not loyal—evil appointment is manifest—wolf-tigers hold office—already sunk in slander—again exposed what was spoken—crime none heavier.
translations.idiomatic: I have been disloyal by exposing this villain; jackals hold office and my charge rings louder—no guilt weighs more than mine.

sentences.zh: 請收惲、延，以明好惡。

translations.literal: Request seize Yun and Yan—to clarify good and evil.
translations.idiomatic: Arrest Yun and Yan together and let the court judge good from evil.

sentences.zh: 』歙曰：『是重吾過也。

translations.literal: ' She said: 'This doubles my fault.
translations.idiomatic: She answered: "That would only deepen my shame.

sentences.zh: 』遂不宴而罷。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon did not banquet but dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: He broke off the feast.

sentences.zh: 惲歸府，稱病，延亦自退。

translations.literal: Yun returned mansion—claimed illness—Yan also self withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: Yun went home feigning illness; Yan resigned as well.

sentences.zh: 鄭敬素與惲厚，見其言許歙，乃相招去，曰：『子廷爭繇延，君猶不納。

translations.literal: Zheng Jing always with Yun thick—saw his words reproved She—thereupon mutually invited to leave, saying: 'You court disputed Yao Yan—lord still not accepted.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Jing, Yun's friend, said: "You challenged Yan in open court and the governor still would not listen.

sentences.zh: 延今雖去，其勢必還。

translations.literal: Yan now although gone—his trend certainly will return.
translations.idiomatic: Yan may leave but he will surely come back.

sentences.zh: 直心無諱，誠三代之道。

translations.literal: Straight mind without taboo—truly three dynasties' Way.
translations.idiomatic: Plain speech without concealment was the way of the Three Ages.

sentences.zh: 然道不同者不相為謀，吾不能忍見子有不容君之危，盍去之乎！

translations.literal: Yet those whose Way differs do not plot together—I cannot bear seeing you have danger of not being tolerated by lord—why not leave!
translations.idiomatic: Men who differ in principle cannot scheme together—I cannot watch you risk banishment—let us go!

sentences.zh: 』惲曰：『孟軻以強其君之所不能為忠，量其君之所不能為賊。

translations.literal: ' Yun said: 'Meng Ke took forcing his lord what he cannot as loyalty—measuring his lord what he cannot as injury.
translations.idiomatic: Yun quoted Mencius: "To urge on your lord past his strength is loyalty; to demand what he cannot give is harm.

sentences.zh: 惲業已強之矣。

translations.literal: Yun's enterprise already forced him.
translations.idiomatic: I have already pressed him hard.

sentences.zh: 障君於朝，既有其直，而不死職，罪也。

translations.literal: Blocking lord at court—already having his straight—yet not dying in duty—crime.
translations.idiomatic: Having challenged him in open court, to flee office now would itself be a crime.

sentences.zh: 延退而惲又去，不可。

translations.literal: Yan withdraws and Yun again leaves—cannot.
translations.idiomatic: If Yan goes and I follow, it cannot be done.

sentences.zh: 』敬乃獨隱於弋陽山中，居數月，歙果復召延，惲於是乃去，從敬止，漁釣自娛，留數十日。

translations.literal: ' Jing then alone hid in Yiyang mountains—dwelt several months—She indeed again summoned Yan—Yun thereupon left—followed Jing's stopping—fishing angling self-amused—remained several tens days.
translations.idiomatic: Jing hid alone in the Yiyang hills; months later She recalled Yan, and Yun joined Jing to fish and idle away weeks.

sentences.zh: 惲誌在從政，既乃喟然而嘆，謂敬曰：『天生俊士，以為人也。

translations.literal: Yun's intent was in following government—thereupon sighed greatly—told Jing, saying: 'Heaven produces handsome gentlemen—to act for others.
translations.idiomatic: Yun still hungered for office and sighed to Jing: "Heaven breeds heroes for the world's use.

sentences.zh: 鳥獸不可與同群，子從我為伊、呂乎？

translations.literal: Birds and beasts cannot with same flock—you follow me to be Yi and Lü?
translations.idiomatic: Will you roam with beasts or follow me as Yi Yin and Lü Wang?

sentences.zh: 將為巢、許，而父老堯、舜乎？

translations.literal: Will be Chao and Xu—yet father elders Yao and Shun?
translations.idiomatic: Or hide like Chaofu and Xu You while I serve Yao and Shun?

sentences.zh: 』敬曰：『吾足矣。

translations.literal: ' Jing said: 'Your servant is enough.
translations.idiomatic: Jing said: "I have had enough.

sentences.zh: 初從生步重華於南野，謂來歸為松子，今幸得全軀樹類，還奉墳墓，盡學問道，雖不從政，施之有政，是亦為政也。

translations.literal: First followed raw student pacing Chonghua at southern wild—called coming home pine seed—now fortunately obtained preserving body tree kind—returning serving tombs—exhausting learning asking Way—although not following government—applying it has government—this also is government.
translations.idiomatic: Long ago I walked with Shun in the southern wastes and dreamed of becoming the immortal Chisongzi; now I am content to keep this body, tend my parents' graves, and study—though I hold no office, teaching is also ruling.

sentences.zh: 吾年耄矣，安得從子？

translations.literal: Your servant's years aged—how obtain following you?
translations.idiomatic: I am too old to march with you.

sentences.zh: 子勉正性命，勿勞神以害生。

translations.literal: You strive to correct nature and life—do not weary spirit to harm life.
translations.idiomatic: Tend your nature and spare your soul.

sentences.zh: 』惲於是告別而去。

translations.literal: ' Yun thereupon took leave and left.
translations.idiomatic: Yun said farewell and departed.

sentences.zh: 敬字次都，清誌高世，光武連征不到。

translations.literal: Jing's style was Cidu—pure intent high age—Guangwu repeatedly summoned did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Jing, courtesy name Cidu, kept aloof; Guangwu summoned him many times in vain.

sentences.zh: 惲遂客居江夏教授，郡舉孝廉，為上東城門候。

translations.literal: Yun thereupon lodged guest teaching at Jiangxia—commandery recommended Filial and Incorrupt—became Upper Eastern Gate Watcher.
translations.idiomatic: Yun taught in Jiangxia, was recommended as filial and honest, and became warden of the Upper East Gate.

sentences.zh: 帝嘗出獵，車駕夜還，惲拒關不開。

translations.literal: Emperor once went hunting—carriage returned night—Yun refused gate not open.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor returned from a hunt by night; Yun would not open the gate.

sentences.zh: 帝令從者見面於門間。

translations.literal: Emperor ordered followers show face at gate gap.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu told his escort to show themselves in the gate light.

sentences.zh: 惲曰：『火明遼遠』。

translations.literal: Yun said: 'Fire bright far-reaching.'
translations.idiomatic: Yun called back: "The light is too distant to tell who you are."

sentences.zh: 遂不受詔。

translations.literal: Thereupon did not accept edict.
translations.idiomatic: He refused the order until sure.

sentences.zh: 帝乃回從東中門入。

translations.literal: Emperor then returned following Eastern Middle Gate to enter.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor detoured through the Eastern Middle Gate.

sentences.zh: 明日，惲上書諫曰：『昔文王不敢槃於遊田，以萬人惟憂。

translations.literal: Next day—Yun upward memorial remonstrated, saying: 'Formerly King Wen did not dare indulge in roaming hunt—ten thousand people's only worry.
translations.idiomatic: Next day he memorialized: "King Wen would not sport in the fields while his people suffered.

sentences.zh: 而陛下遠獵山林，夜以繼晝，其於社稷宗廟何？

translations.literal: Yet Your Majesty far hunts mountains and forests—night succeeding day—what of altars of soil and grain and ancestral temple?
translations.idiomatic: Yet Your Majesty hunts deep in the hills day and night—what of the altars and shrines?

sentences.zh: 暴虎馮河，未至之戒，誠小臣所竊憂也。

translations.literal: Naked tiger ford river—not arrived's warning—truly petty minister's private worry.
translations.idiomatic: Rushing tigers and fording rivers—the Classic warns before disaster—I tremble for you.

sentences.zh: 』書奏，賜布百匹，貶東中門候為參封尉。

translations.literal: ' Document submitted—bestowed cloth hundred bolts—demoted Eastern Middle Gate Watcher to Shenfeng Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent silk but demoted the eastern middle gate warden to commandant of Shenfeng.

sentences.zh: 後令惲授皇太子《韓詩》，侍講殿中。

translations.literal: Later ordered Yun teach Imperial Heir Han's Odes—attending lecture in palace hall.
translations.idiomatic: He was later told to instruct the heir apparent in the Han Odes inside the palace.

sentences.zh: 及郭皇後廢。

translations.literal: When Empress Guo was deposed.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Guo was set aside.

sentences.zh: 惲乃言於帝曰：『臣聞夫婦之好，父不能得之於子，況臣能得之於君乎？

translations.literal: Yun then spoke to emperor, saying: 'Your servant has heard husband-wife's fondness—father cannot obtain it from son—how much more servant obtain from lord?
translations.idiomatic: Yun told the emperor: "Even a father cannot command love between husband and wife—how dare a subject lecture his ruler?

sentences.zh: 是臣所不敢言。

translations.literal: This is what your servant dare not speak.
translations.idiomatic: That is why I held my tongue.

sentences.zh: 雖然，願陛下念其可否之計，無令天下有議社稷而已。

translations.literal: Although thus—wish Your Majesty ponder its yes-no plan—do not order all under Heaven have discussion of altars of soil and grain only.
translations.idiomatic: Still I beg you to weigh the matter lest the realm rumble about the fate of the state.

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『惲善恕己量主，知我必不有所左右而輕天也。

translations.literal: ' Emperor said: 'Yun good at forgiving self measuring lord—knows I certainly will not have left-right and slight Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered: "Yun knows how to put himself in my place—he trusts I would not twist Heaven's intent for private ends.

sentences.zh: 』後既廢，而太子意不自安，惲乃說太子曰：『久處疑位，上違孝道，下近危殆。

translations.literal: ' Later already deposed—yet heir apparent's intent not self secure—Yun then persuaded heir apparent, saying: 'Long dwelling doubtful position—above violates filial way—below approaches peril.
translations.idiomatic: After the empress fell the heir lived in fear; Yun urged him: "To linger in doubt offends filial piety above and courts ruin below.

sentences.zh: 昔高宗明君，吉甫賢臣，及有纖介，放逐孝子。

translations.literal: Formerly Gaozong bright lord—Jifu worthy minister—yet had minute flaw—banished filial son.
translations.idiomatic: King Gaozong and Jifu were paragons, yet the slightest fault sent the dutiful crown prince into exile.

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，母以子貴。

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn's meaning—mother by son noble.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn Annals say the mother rises with the son.

sentences.zh: 太子宜因左右及諸皇子引愆退身，奉養母氏，以明聖教，不背所生。

translations.literal: Heir apparent ought through attendants and various imperial sons lead fault withdraw body—supporting nurturing mother's clan—to clarify sage teaching—not betraying those who bore.
translations.idiomatic: Withdraw through your brothers, serve your mother's kin, and prove you do not abandon her who bore you.

sentences.zh: 』太子從之，帝竟聽許。

translations.literal: ' Heir followed—emperor ultimately listened allowed.
translations.idiomatic: The heir obeyed and the emperor consented.

sentences.zh: 惲再遷長沙太守。

translations.literal: Yun twice promoted Governor of Changsha.
translations.idiomatic: Yun rose twice to governor of Changsha.

sentences.zh: 先是，長沙有孝子古初，遭父喪未葬，鄰人失火，初匍匐柩上，以身扞火，火為之滅。

translations.literal: Before this—Changsha had filial son Gu Chu—met father mourning not yet buried—neighbor lost fire—Chu crawled on coffin—used body shield fire—fire for this extinguished.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Gu Chu of Changsha had lain on his father's unburied coffin to stop a neighbor's fire and smothered the flames.

sentences.zh: 惲甄異之，以為首舉。

translations.literal: Yun distinguished him extraordinary—took as first recommendation.
translations.idiomatic: Yun singled him out and ranked him first among recommendations.

sentences.zh: 後坐事左轉芒長，又免歸，避地教授，著書八篇。

translations.literal: Later sat affair turned left Mang Magistrate—again dismissed returned—avoided ground teaching—composed writings eight pieces.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was demoted to magistrate of Mang, dismissed, taught privately, and wrote eight essays.

sentences.zh: 以病卒。

translations.literal: Because illness died.
translations.idiomatic: He died of illness.

sentences.zh: 子壽。

translations.literal: Son Shou.
translations.idiomatic: His son was Zhi Shou.

sentences.zh: 壽字伯孝，善文章，以廉能稱，舉孝廉，稍遷冀州刺史。

translations.literal: Shou's style was Boxiao—good at literary compositions—by incorrupt capability praised—recommended Filial and Incorrupt—gradually promoted Jizhou Inspector.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Shou, courtesy name Boxiao, excelled at prose and was known for honesty; recommended as filial and honest, he rose to inspector of Jizhou.

sentences.zh: 時，冀部屬郡多封諸王。

translations.literal: At the time Ji province's subordinate commanderies many enfeoffed various kings.
