translations.idiomatic: Many commanderies in Ji hosted imperial princes.

sentences.zh: 賓客放縱，類不檢節，壽案察之，無所容貸。

translations.literal: Guests and retainers unrestrained—mostly not restrained rule—Shou investigated them—nowhere tolerated loan.
translations.idiomatic: Their retainers ran wild; Shou investigated relentlessly and granted no indulgence.

sentences.zh: 乃使部從事專住王國，又徙督郵舍王宮外，動靜失得，即時騎驛言上奏王罪及劾傅相，於是藩國畏懼，並為遵節。

translations.literal: Thereupon ordered branch clerk specially stationed king's state—again moved supervising clerk's lodging outside king's palace—movement still gain loss—immediately mounted relay reported upward memorializing king's crime and impeaching tutor and chancellor—thereupon feudal states feared—and all obeyed regulation.
translations.idiomatic: He stationed aides in each kingdom, moved the supervising clerks outside princely gates, and reported every fault by courier—soon every fief trembled and obeyed the law.

sentences.zh: 視事三年，冀土肅清。

translations.literal: Handling affairs three years—Ji territory solemnly pure.
translations.idiomatic: In three years he cleared Ji province.

sentences.zh: 三遷尚書令。

translations.literal: Thrice promoted Masters of Writing Supervisor.
translations.idiomatic: Three promotions brought him to supervisor of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 朝廷每有疑議，常獨進見。

translations.literal: Court whenever had doubtful deliberation—often alone advanced audience.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever court debated a doubt, he often saw the emperor alone.

sentences.zh: 肅宗奇其智策，擢為京兆尹。

translations.literal: Emperor Suzong marveled his wisdom strategy—promoted to Metropolitan Governor.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang admired his counsel and named him governor of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 郡多強豪，奸暴不禁。

translations.literal: Commandery many powerful magnates—treacherous violence not forbidden.
translations.idiomatic: The capital teemed with bullies the law could not check.

sentences.zh: 三輔素聞壽在冀州，皆懷震竦，各相檢敕，莫敢幹犯。

translations.literal: Three auxiliaries always heard Shou at Jizhou—all cherished trembling fear—each mutually inspected restrain—none dared offend violate.
translations.idiomatic: The three capital districts had heard of his work in Jizhou—everyone quaked and minded their conduct.

sentences.zh: 壽雖威嚴，而推誠下吏，皆願效死，莫有欺者。

translations.literal: Although Shou awesome stern—yet extended sincerity below clerks—all wished effect death—none had deceit.
translations.idiomatic: Though fierce with magnates, he dealt honestly with subordinates; they would die for him and none deceived him.

sentences.zh: 以公事免。

translations.literal: Because public affairs dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: He lost office over an administrative matter.

sentences.zh: 復征為尚書僕射。

translations.literal: Again summoned Masters of Writing Deputy Supervisor.
translations.idiomatic: He was recalled as masters-of-writing vice-director.

sentences.zh: 是時，大將軍竇憲以外戚之寵，威傾天下。

translations.literal: At this time Grand General Dou Xian by outer kin's favor—awe tilted all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Grand General Dou Xian, favored as imperial in-law, overshadowed the realm.

sentences.zh: 憲嘗使門生賫書詣壽，有所請托，壽即送詔獄。

translations.literal: Xian once ordered disciple carried document visit Shou—had request entrustment—Shou immediately sent edict prison.
translations.idiomatic: Dou once sent a follower with a note soliciting favor; Shou jailed the man at once.

sentences.zh: 前後上書陳憲驕恣，引王莽以誡國家。

translations.literal: Before after upward memorial stated Xian arrogant self-indulgent—cited Wang Mang to warn state.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly memorialized against Dou's arrogance, citing Wang Mang as a warning.

sentences.zh: 是時，憲征匈奴，海內供其役費，百憲及其弟篤、景並起第宅，驕奢非法，百姓苦之。

translations.literal: At this time—Xian campaigned Xiongnu—within seas supplied his service costs—Dou Xian and his younger brothers Du and Jing together raised mansions—arrogant extravagant beyond law—common people suffered them.
translations.idiomatic: While Dou campaigned against the Xiongnu at public expense, he and his brothers Du and Jing raised mansions in lavish defiance of statute—the people groaned under the burden.

sentences.zh: 壽以府臧空虛，軍旅未休，遂因朝會譏刺憲等，厲音正色，辭旨甚切。

translations.literal: Shou because treasury storehouses empty void—armies camps not resting—thereupon at court meeting ridicule stabbed Xian and others—severe voice correct color—word intent very cutting.
translations.idiomatic: With the treasury empty and armies still in the field, Shou mocked Dou at court in harsh rebukes.

sentences.zh: 憲怒，陷壽以買公田誹謗，下吏當誅。

translations.literal: Xian angry—trapped Shou with buying public field slander—handed to clerks ought execute.
translations.idiomatic: Dou retaliated with charges about buying public land and demanded execution.

sentences.zh: 侍御史何敞上疏理之曰：『臣聞聖王辟四門，開四聰，延直言之路，下不諱之詔，立敢諫之旗，聽歌謠於路，爭臣七人，以自鑒照，考知政理，違失人心，輒改更之，故天人並應，傳福無窮。

translations.literal: Attendant Censor He Chang upward memorial reasoned him, saying: 'Your servant has heard sage kings open four gates—open four hearings—extend straight speech's road—issue no-taboo edicts—raise daring remonstrance banner—listen songs ballads on road—contending ministers seven persons—to self mirror reflect—examine know government pattern—contrary lose human hearts—then change replace them—therefore Heaven and man together respond—transmit blessing without limit.
translations.idiomatic: Attendant censor He Chang argued: "Sage kings kept channels open for criticism and swift reform—Heaven and earth answered with blessing.

sentences.zh: 臣伏見尚書僕射郅壽坐於臺上，與諸尚書論擊匈奴，言議過差，及上書請買公田，遂系獄考劾大不敬。

translations.literal: Your servant humbly sees Masters of Writing Deputy Supervisor Zhi Shou sitting on terrace—with various Masters of Writing discussing striking Xiongnu—speech deliberation excessive difference—and upward memorial requesting buy public field—thereupon bound prison investigate impeach great disrespect.
translations.idiomatic: He noted that Shou had debated the Xiongnu campaign heatedly and sought to buy public land—hardly crimes worthy of prison.

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為壽機密近臣，匡救為職。

translations.literal: Your servant foolishly thinks Shou secret confidential near minister—correct save is duty.
translations.idiomatic: Shou stands at the emperor's side to right wrongs.

sentences.zh: 若懷默不言，其罪當誅。

translations.literal: If cherish silence not speak—his crime ought execute.
translations.idiomatic: Silence in crisis would deserve death.

sentences.zh: 今壽違眾正議，以安宗廟，豈其私邪？

translations.literal: Now Shou opposes crowd upright deliberation—to pacify ancestral temple—is it his private bias?
translations.idiomatic: Now he speaks against the crowd to protect the shrines—how is that private interest?

sentences.zh: 又臺閣平事，分爭可否，雖唐、虞之隆，三代之盛，猶謂諤諤以昌，不以誹謗為罪。

translations.literal: Moreover terrace pavilion level affairs—divide argue yes no—although Tang and Yu's elevation—three dynasties' splendor—still called blunt blunt thereby flourish—not take slander as crime.
translations.idiomatic: Even in Yao and Shun's age ministers wrangled openly—truth flourishes when frank speech is no crime.

sentences.zh: 請買公田，人情細過，可裁隱忍。

translations.literal: Request buy public field—human feeling small fault—can cut conceal tolerate.
translations.idiomatic: Buying land is a venial fault that mercy may cover.

sentences.zh: 壽若被誅，臣恐天下以為國家橫罪忠直，賊傷和氣，忤逆陰陽。

translations.literal: Shou if executed—your servant fears all under Heaven will think state unjustly punishes loyal straight—injures harmony vapor—disobeys yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: Execute Shou and the world will say the court murders loyalty and wounds Heaven's balance.

sentences.zh: 臣所以敢犯嚴威，不避夷滅，觸死瞽言，非為壽也。

translations.literal: Your servant's reason for daring to violate awesome might—not avoiding extinction—touching death blind speech—is not for Shou.
translations.idiomatic: I brave your wrath and risk annihilation to speak though death closes my eyes—not for Zhi Shou's sake alone.

sentences.zh: 忠臣盡節，以死為歸。

translations.literal: Loyal ministers exhaust integrity—take death as homecoming.
translations.idiomatic: A loyal subject serves to the limit and accepts death as his destination.

sentences.zh: 臣雖不知壽，度其甘心安之。

translations.literal: Your servant although does not know Shou—measures he willingly minds it.
translations.idiomatic: I scarcely know Shou, yet I believe he would bear the sentence without regret.

sentences.zh: 誠不欲聖朝行誹謗之誅，以傷晏晏之化，杜塞忠直，垂譏無窮。

translations.literal: Truly not wishing sagely court to carry out slander's execution—to harm gentle gentle transformation—to block loyal straight—to hang ridicule without limit.
translations.idiomatic: I cannot bear to see the dynasty punish honest speech, wound mild rule, choke off loyal men, and invite endless shame.

sentences.zh: 臣敞謬豫機密，言所不宜，罪名明白，當填牢獄，先壽僵仆，萬死有餘。

translations.literal: Your servant Chang wrongly participated secret confidential—spoke what was not fitting—crime name clear bright—ought fill prison—before Shou fall dead—ten thousand deaths have surplus.
translations.idiomatic: I intruded on secrets I should not know and spoke out of turn—my guilt is plain; I belong in chains before Shou falls—death ten thousand times over would not suffice.

sentences.zh: 』書奏，壽得減死，論徙合浦。

translations.literal: ' Document submitted—Shou obtained reduced death—sentenced relocate Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial went up, Shou's sentence was commuted from death to exile in Hepu.

sentences.zh: 未行，自殺，家屬得歸鄉裏。

translations.literal: Not yet departed—self killed—family dependents obtained return village.
translations.idiomatic: Before he set out he took his own life; his family was allowed home.

translations.literal: [Eulogy]
translations.idiomatic: Eulogy

sentences.zh: 贊曰：鮑永沈吟，晚乃歸正。

translations.literal: The eulogy says: Bao Yong sank and pondered—in later years then returned to rectitude.
translations.idiomatic: The summation runs: Bao Yong hesitated long before he turned to the right path.

sentences.zh: 誌達義全，先號後慶。

translations.literal: Intent reached righteousness complete—first cry afterward blessing.
translations.idiomatic: His purpose matched duty and brought him through lament to blessing.

sentences.zh: 申屠對策，郅惲上書。

translations.literal: Shentu answered policy questions—Zhi Yun upward memorialized.
translations.idiomatic: Shentu Gang answered the examination; Zhi Yun laid memorials before the throne.

sentences.zh: 有道雖直，無道不愚。

translations.literal: Having Way although straight—without Way not foolish.
translations.idiomatic: Under a rightful Way they were blunt; even in a wrongful age they were never fools.
